phrases.french: La basilique est-elle ouverte à la visite ?
phrases.english: Is the basilica open for visitors?
phrases.phonetic: lah bah-ZEEK eh-TEL oo-VAIR-tuh ah lah vee-ZEET
phrases.context: Saint-Remi is the oldest church in Reims and a UNESCO World Heritage Site alongside the cathedral. It contains the tomb of Saint Remi, who baptised Clovis in 498.

phrases.french: Où est le tombeau de Saint Remi ?
phrases.english: Where is the tomb of Saint Remi?
phrases.phonetic: oo eh luh tom-BOH duh san ruh-MEE
phrases.context: The elaborate 19th-century tomb of Saint Remi is in the choir — the saint who baptised the first Christian King of France, Clovis, in 498 AD.

tips: Often less crowded than the cathedral — a more peaceful and equally impressive visit
The basilica dates to 1049, making it older than Notre-Dame de Paris
The attached museum (Musée Saint-Remi) covers Gallo-Roman Reims and is worth an hour
